checks.description: Checks if 'session.resetByType' is defined but omits entries for known session types, which could lead to unexpected default behavior.
checks.remediation: Your 'session.resetByType' configuration is missing entries for one or more session types. This can cause them to fall back to the application's default reset policy (e.g., 'daily'), leading to unexpected data loss after an upgrade. It is strongly recommended to explicitly define a policy for all types: 'direct', 'group', and 'thread'.
